Windows Remote Management (WinRM)

Windows Remote Management (WinRM) is Microsoft's implementation of the WS-Management (WS-Man) protocol, providing a built-in framework for remote Windows administration. It enables authenticated users to execute commands, manage Windows components, modify the registry and services, and establish PowerShell Remoting sessions over HTTP (TCP 5985) or HTTPS (TCP 5986). WinRM is commonly accessed through WinRS or PowerShell and is frequently used for legitimate administration and lateral movement.

Requirements

  • Valid local or domain credentials.
  • Membership in the Administrators or Remote Management Users group on the target host.
  • The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must be running and configured.
  • Network connectivity to the target.
  • Required ports must be accessible: TCP 5985 (HTTP) or TCP 5986 (HTTPS).
  • Firewall rules must allow WinRM traffic.

Resources

MITRE ATT&CK T1021.006
